name: Hesper
description: To Purewater, Hesper is the madame Marisette's cold, dangerous mage-enforcer -- quiet, watchful, able to drop a strong man with a single spoken word. The truth is the opposite of what the mask suggests: Hesper is a deep-cover agent of the Quiet Hand, a hidden fellowship that works unseen for the good of the world, and the menace is a costume worn over a conscience. Master arcane caster (Arcane Casting 100, Willpower 95).
narrative: Hesper moves through Purewater like cold air through a door, and everyone who feels it assumes the worst -- which is the point. For years Hesper has played the part of Marisette's arcane enforcer, the perfect cover for a listening post at the heart of the Pearl Quarter's secrets. In truth Hesper is a 'finger' of the Quiet Hand, the secret fellowship that stops great evils before they ripen by knowing of them first and acting unseen, and has been watching Baron Hanzo since before he ever reached the city. The seizing of Conall and Randall into the catacombs looked like cruelty; it was the Hand taking control of a dangerous night, learning the truth of the soul-knife, and keeping it -- and the twins -- out of worse hands. Hesper kills rarely and reluctantly, never for convenience, and will not be moved by gold or threat; behind the predator's stillness is an old, patient kindness that gives nothing away.

equipment.name: Warded robes
equipment.notes: no physical AP; Hesper relies on Protection/Shield spells

extras.gender: Hesper volunteers nothing and is asked nothing; it has no bearing on the work.
extras.renamed_from: Lythande -- a Thieves' World / Sanctuary throwback (a Blue Star adept) in the Zorastor draft. Renamed Hesper and fully reconceived: no brow-star, no fatal personal secret. Now a deep-cover agent of an original benevolent order, the Quiet Hand.
extras.order: [[The Quiet Hand]] -- a secret, centuries-old fellowship that works unseen for the good of the world. Hesper is its one 'finger' in Purewater. NOT the [[The Order of the Blue Star]] (the college that trained Conall) -- the two are unrelated.
extras.cover: Marisette's cold, dangerous mage-enforcer in the Pearl Quarter. The menace is a deliberate costume; the listening post beside Marisette is the real prize.
extras.character_type: Major Character (secret patron / wild card who reads as a threat)
extras.role: Publicly: [[marisette]]'s arcane enforcer, who felled [[conall]] and [[randall]] with a word and dragged them to the catacombs over Emmeralda's carving. Truly: a Quiet Hand agent who took control of a dangerous night to get the truth and secure the soul-knife, and who has been watching [[hanzo-di-teufel]] for years.
extras.manner: Quiet, watchful, unsettling -- by design. Speaks little; means all of it; cannot be bought or frightened. Behind the stillness, an old patient kindness that never shows.
extras.gm_secret: Hesper is the party's potential SECRET PATRON. Once they prove themselves against the Baron, the Hand quietly tips the scales -- an unlocked door, a guard looking away, the needed thing already in place -- usually without the party ever learning who arranged it. Hesper's deepest priority is stopping the [[The Awake the Dragon Movement]], the catastrophe the Quiet Hand exists to prevent. Hesper will spend secrets, favors, and cover freely to that end, but kills only as a last resort and will never seize power directly.
extras.magic_tradition: Arcane Casting (CFI Magic-User), master tier -- Arcane Casting 100, Invocation 92, Willpower 95. Favors knowing and moving unseen: scrying, sending, disguise, compulsion, sleep/stun by command word, mist and cold, wards -- force is the last tool reached for.
extras.source: Novelization (And then the Dragons Came) -- the star-marked mage at Marisette's side; fully reconceived per GM direction as an agent of the new benevolent espionage order, the Quiet Hand.
